example_prompt: Push a Togocel or Moov Togo mobile money debit request to a customer's phone via Qosic.

msisdn.description: Customer phone number in international format with no '+' (Togo = 228xxxxxxxx).

amount.description: Amount as a string, in XOF, no decimals. Example: '100'.

transref.description: Your unique merchant transaction reference. Must be unique across all your transactions.

clientid.description: Operator-specific Client ID from your Qosic dashboard (Settings -> API Keys & Callback URL). Different per operator: e.g. TG_CREPiNL for Togocel, TG_CREPCaT for Moov Togo.

network.description: Mobile money network. TOGOCEL = Togocel / Yas. MOOV = Moov Togo. Determines which Qosic URL is called (TOGOCEL -> /tm/v1/, MOOV -> /tg/v1/).

responsecode.description: Outcome code. '00' = successful synchronous debit. '01' = pending (customer must validate the USSD push). Code is a STRING, not an integer.

responsemsg.description: Human-readable status. Common values: 'SUCCESSFUL', 'PENDING', 'demande en cours', plus error labels.

transref.description: Your merchant transaction reference echoed back.

serviceref.description: Qosic-side reference. Can be null at initiation (filled in once Qosic finishes routing), and is set to null/'0' for Togo and for unknown transactions.

comment.description: Free-form comment. Observed values include 'payment' for collections, null otherwise.

responsecode.description: Error code. '02' / '-1' / '-2' = failed. '401' = auth failed. '529' = insufficient funds. '527' = network issue. '515' = wrong network for that number. '580' = receiver limit exceeded. '849' = validation failed. '682' = internal error. Many other codes documented at docs.qosic.com.

gotchas: responsecode '01' (pending) is the NORMAL initial response — the customer still has to validate the USSD push. Poll check_payment_status with the SAME transref until you reach a terminal state ('00' success, '02'/'-1' failure).
Successful synchronous responses return HTTP 202 (Accepted), not 200. Treat both 200 and 202 as 'request accepted'; the real outcome is in the JSON body's responsecode.
serviceref can be null on the initial response (filled in later) and is generally null/'0' for Togo. Do not assume it is a non-null string — type it as string | null.
The URL depends on the network: TOGOCEL -> /QosicBridge/tm/v1/requestpayment, MOOV -> /QosicBridge/tg/v1/requestpayment. The canonical_example handles this switch; do not hard-code one URL for both.

Each operator has a DIFFERENT clientid (visible in your dashboard, Settings -> API Keys & Callback URL). Sending the Togocel clientid to the Moov URL (or vice-versa) fails. Manage them as separate env vars (e.g. QOSIC_CLIENT_ID_TOGOCEL, QOSIC_CLIENT_ID_MOOV).
Auth is HTTP Basic with merchant username:password (NOT the clientid). Username/password are in the dashboard. Set QOSIC_USERNAME and QOSIC_PASSWORD env vars.

Sandbox is on staging.qosic.net:9010 over HTTPS. The public docs say http:// but the server now redirects/rejects plain HTTP with a 400; always use https://staging.qosic.net:9010. Public test credentials: USR01 / YG739G5XFVPYYV4ADJVW with the shared sandbox clientid MTNTEST (or MOOVTEST). These are Qosic's generic sandbox test clientids — in production each Togo operator (Togocel, Moov) has its own clientid from your dashboard. The sandbox does NOT actually send USSD pushes; production only.
[MUST TELL USER] Configure your server IP in Qosic dashboard -> IP Whitelist before calling any API in production. Requests from non-whitelisted IPs are rejected.
transref must be globally unique. Reusing a transref returns an error and breaks idempotency on your side.
